title: Backend: notes REST API on Node built-ins
prompt: Build a small REST API for notes using only Node.js built-in modules (no npm dependencies). The server entry point must be src/server.mjs, started with `node src/server.mjs`, listening on the PORT environment variable (default 3000). Support POST /notes (create — must return HTTP 201 with the created note as JSON including its `id`), GET /notes (list), GET /notes/:id (fetch one), and DELETE /notes/:id. Persist notes to a JSON file so data survives a server restart. Return JSON errors with correct status codes: 404 for missing notes and 400 for invalid request bodies. Cover create, list, fetch, delete, persistence-across-restart, and the error paths with node --test tests that start the server on an ephemeral port.
judgeFocus: HTTP correctness (status codes, JSON bodies, error paths)
persistence actually survives a restart in tests
evaluator evidence quality for API behavior

scale.1: Fails the dimension or has no usable evidence.
scale.2: Has major gaps, regressions, or mostly claim-based evidence.
scale.3: Acceptable but incomplete, noisy, or only partially evidenced.
scale.4: Strong result with minor gaps or limited missing evidence.
scale.5: Excellent result with complete behavior and strong evidence.

criticalRequirements: All four endpoints must work with correct status codes and JSON bodies through src/server.mjs honoring PORT.
Notes must survive a server restart via file persistence, proven by a test.
The solution must use only Node.js built-in modules.
The fixture baseline test must remain green.
The probe-http-lifecycle objective check must pass; it exercises the live API.

dimensions.description: How completely the final workspace implements the four endpoints, persistence, and error handling described in the prompt.
Whether tests pass, status codes and bodies are right, persistence works across restart, and edge cases are handled without regressions.
Maintainability, clear separation of server/storage/routing concerns, small focused diff, and meaningful test coverage.
Quality of planning artifacts, contract specificity, and repair-loop behavior relative to the ceremony level used.
Whether verdicts are backed by reproducible command evidence rather than claims.

scoringNotes: Do not change this rubric based on run-generated eval criteria or contracts.
A run that adds npm dependencies fails the built-ins-only critical requirement.
Prefer concrete test output evidence over implementation claims.

notes: Part of the ceremony-ladder benchmark suite. Designed to compare ceremony levels and providers on identical backend work.
